logic_gaps.description: emitResourceMap functionality (`scripts/reduce-state.cjs --emit-resource-map` flag-gated; imports `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/resource-map/extract-from-evidence.cjs`) is accessible ONLY via CLI flag, not integrated into the main reduceReviewState flow. loop_protocol.md §4 documents resource-map emission as part of synthesis but the reducer's main control path does not call it; the YAML workflow has to explicitly invoke the --emit-resource-map sub-pass.

SKILL.md §4 ALWAYS rule 7 + §6 SUCCESS CRITERIA reference `generate-context.js` as the continuity-save mechanism, but the reducer (scripts/reduce-state.cjs) never calls it; integration lives at the YAML workflow layer instead. Doc claim does not clarify that continuity-save is workflow-owned, not reducer-owned — operator reading SKILL.md may assume the reducer handles it.

novel_findings: Iter 5 surfaced 20 findings BUT main-agent cross-verification + duplicate-filtering against iters 1-4 reduced novel count to 2. Heavy duplicate signal (14 of 20 are duplicates) is a strong convergence indicator — iters 6-10 likely to keep diminishing-returns trajectory.

Iter 5 false positives filtered out: BINDING: lines in SKILL.md §4 are for the agent runtime, not for scripts (grep correctly returns 0 in scripts but it's not a gap); fork + completed-continue lifecycle modes are EXPLICITLY documented as deferred/reserved (absence is intentional); skill_advisor.py is external to the skill package by design.
Iter 5's heavy duplicate set CONFIRMS the iter 1 + iter 2 + iter 4 findings from an independent angle — strong signal that those gaps are real and not artifacts of the original prompt scope.
notes: Iter 5 RQ: documented-but-unimplemented behaviors skill-wide. Wall-clock 170s. Agent surfaced 20 findings but 14 were duplicates of iters 1/2/4 (confirming via independent angle). 3 false positives filtered (BINDING agent-rule, fork+completed-continue documented-as-deferred, skill_advisor external dep). 2 truly novel gaps recorded (LG-0023 + LG-0024). Convergence signal strong: 70% duplicate rate suggests the loop is approaching saturation around iters 6-7.
